reasoning: The baseline 360-day average of 349.0 'Violence against civilians' events (from mid-April 2024 to mid-April 2025) contains the October 2024 Brazilian municipal elections. Political violence traditionally spikes during Brazilian elections, artificially inflating the 12-month average relative to a typical 'baseline' non-election month. Furthermore, overall violent deaths in Brazil decreased by roughly 5% throughout 2024, indicating a macro-level downward trend. Event count distributions of this type are typically right-skewed, meaning the median is lower than the mean; most months fall below the 12-month average, punctuated by occasional high-side outliers. Because the future resolution months (April, May, July, and October 2025) are largely outside significant electoral campaigning or identified new structural spikes, typical monthly counts should be lower than 349. We assign a probability below 50% for all resolution dates, moving closer to 0.50 (maximum uncertainty) for the October date due to the longer forecast horizon.
